description:
## Duplication

The "use previous feed data if not failed, otherwise build an error feed" pattern is **duplicated 4 times** within a single method (`fetch_single_feed_with_timeout`, lines 192–219), and an additional instance exists in `fetch_single_feed_in_background` (lines 145–152).

Each duplicate follows the shape:

```crystal
if previous_feed_data && !previous_feed_data.failed?
  Log.for("quickheadlines.feed").info { "... using cached data ..." }
  previous_feed_data
else
  FeedFetcher.instance.build_error_feed(feed, "Error: ...")
end
```

## Why it matters

- The 4 copies differ only in the error message and the log line — exactly the kind of copy-paste that drifts over time and introduces subtle inconsistencies (different log keys, different message formats).
- A change to the fallback policy (e.g., "always include a synthetic error feed even when cached data is available, so the UI shows the failure badge") requires editing 4–5 places in the same file.
- This pattern is also a candidate for reuse from `best_available_feed` (line 97), which currently only handles `fetched` + `existing` — not the timeout/exception branches.

## Fix

Extract a single helper:

```crystal
private def self.fallback_feed(feed : Feed, previous : FeedData?, error_message : String, context : String) : FeedData
  if previous && !previous.failed?
    Log.for("quickheadlines.feed").info { "#{context}: using cached data for #{feed.url}" }
    previous
  else
    FeedFetcher.instance.build_error_feed(feed, error_message)
  end
end
```

Then replace all 5 sites:

```crystal
# Before
if previous_feed_data && !previous_feed_data.failed?
  Log.for("quickheadlines.feed").info { "fetch_single_feed_with_timeout: using cached data for #{feed.url}" }
  previous_feed_data
else
  FeedFetcher.instance.build_error_feed(feed, "Error: Fetch timeout after #{timeout_seconds}s")
end

# After
fallback_feed(feed, previous_feed_data,
  "Error: Fetch timeout after #{timeout_seconds}s",
  "fetch_single_feed_with_timeout")
```

## Acceptance

- A single `fallback_feed` (or similarly named) helper exists and is the only path that decides between cached data and synthetic error feed.
- `grep -cE "previous_feed_data && !previous_feed_data.failed\?" src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` returns **at most 1** (the helper itself).
- The 4–5 duplicated blocks are replaced with single helper calls.
- `just nix-build` passes; existing Crystal specs pass.
title: DRY: extract fallback_feed helper to remove 4-way duplication
